title: Even and Odd Components of Signals
summary: This page illustrates the decomposition of a signal x(t) = e^(at) into its even and odd components through Figure 1.24, showing graphical representations of the original signal and its even and odd parts. Example 1.9 demonstrates finding the even and odd components of the complex exponential signal e^(jt), showing that the even component is cos(t) and the odd component is j*sin(t).

figures.caption: Illustration of decomposing a causal exponential signal x(t) = e^{-at}u(t) into its even component x_e(t) and odd component x_o(t).
figures.description: The figure has three time-domain plots with horizontal axis labeled t and vertical axes through t=0, showing decomposition of the causal signal x(t)=e^{-at}u(t). In (a), x(t) is 0 for t<0 and equals e^{-at} for t>0, starting at value 1 at t=0. In (b), the even component x_e(t) is symmetric about t=0 with peak 1/2 at the origin, labeled as (1/2)e^{at} for t<0 and (1/2)e^{-at} for t>0. In (c), the odd component x_o(t) is antisymmetric, equal to (1/2)e^{-at} for t>0 and -(1/2)e^{at} for t<0; students should notice the symmetry properties and that x(t)=x_e(t)+x_o(t).
